title: Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile Song Lyrics Paasa Paravaigal
stanzas:
<div>
Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile Song From Paasa Paravaigal Tamil Movie composed by  Ilaiyaraaja.  This song has beautiful lyrics and shows love between brother and sister, how they both care for each other.

Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile Lyrics is written by Gangai Amaran and the song is sung by KJ Yesudas and KS Chitra.
Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile Lyrics in Thanglish thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile
thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile

unaip paada vendum aayiram

koodi vaazhum kuruvigal

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses the deep love and care between a brother and sister, highlighting their emotional bond.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

In Paasa Paravaigal, the song illustrates a touching moment between siblings, emphasizing family values and affection.

Who are the singers of the song?

The song is sung by the legendary K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chitra.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for this beautiful track was composed by the renowned Ilaiyaraaja.

When was the song released?

The song was released on April 29, 1988.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile, the heartfelt lyrics convey the love and affection shared between a brother and sister. The soothing melody, composed by Ilaiyaraaja, evokes a sense of nostalgia and warmth. As the siblings express their care for each other, listeners are transported to a place of familial love and connection. This timeless classic remains a cherished piece in Tamil music history.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile is a heartwarming melody that beautifully captures the bond between siblings. With the enchanting voices of K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chitra, this song from the film Paasa Paravaigal showcases the magic of Ilaiyaraaja's composition. Experience the nostalgia and love that this timeless classic evokes.
stanzaMeanings: This song from the movie Paasa Paravaigal, composed by Ilaiyaraaja, has beautiful lyrics that express the love between a brother and sister and how they care for each other.

The lyrics of Thenpandi Tamile En Singara Kuyile are written by Gangai Amaran and sung by KJ Yesudas and KS Chitra. The lyrics in Tanglish are: 'thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile isai paadum oru kaaviyam idhu ravi varmavin oviyam paasam enum aalayam unaip paada vendum aayiram'.
thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile
I have no words to praise you. Even looking at you, the love in my eyes doesn't change.
In the group called love, the singing cuckoo desires to sing, and brother, I want to praise you.
I haven't seen the child who has come back (twice).
I see you like a mother; there is no other sight. You should live with turmeric and kumkum, and you should always live.
The body may be different, but life is one. With love, the brother sings; I don't need a temple for that. God, let me live every day.
The little birds that live together sing the songs of love (twice).
I will praise you and celebrate you; all of life is about you.
In every time and place, we should live together; we should live together.
thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile thenpandi tamile en singara kuyile isai paadum oru kaaviyam idhu ravi varmavin oviyam paasam enum aalayam unaip paada vendum aayiram.
